toolCalls.name: update_topic

args.title: Fix Landing Page Auth State
args.summary: I investigated the issue where the landing page was showing the 'Get Started' and 'Sign In' buttons to users even if they were logged in. I updated the `client/app/page.tsx` server component to fetch the user's authentication state via Supabase and pass an `isLoggedIn` boolean to the `LandingPageClient`. Then, I modified `LandingPageClient` to accept this prop and conditionally render a 'Go to Dashboard' button in the navigation header and hero section when the user is logged in. Finally, I successfully ran a build to ensure the code changes compile and type-check properly.
args.strategic_intent: Completed fixing the landing page auth state.

response.output: Strategic Intent: Completed fixing the landing page auth state.

content:
I have successfully updated the landing page so it correctly reflects the authentication state. 

Here is what was changed:
1. Updated `client/app/page.tsx` to check the Supabase auth state and pass an `isLoggedIn` prop to the landing page client component.
2. Modified `client/components/landing/landing-page-client.tsx` to accept the `isLoggedIn` prop.
3. Updated the Navigation Header and the Hero section inside `LandingPageClient` to display a **"Go to Dashboard"** button instead of the **"Sign In"** and **"Get Started"** / **"Start Bookmarking Free"** buttons if the user is already authenticated. 

I've also verified the application compiles and builds successfully with these changes.

thoughts.subject: Confirming Task Completion
